---
title: "Edible Plants Dataset Analisis (TidyTuesday) - Exploration Data Analysis (EDA)"
author: "Leonardo Cárdenas Peralta, Daniel Enrique Molina Sanglade and Enrique Fernandez Salmeron"
date: "April-May 2026"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
---

## Creation of new columns from the old ones

```{r}
edible_plants_clean <- read_csv(here("Data", "edible_plants_cleaned.csv"))
```
```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 3. Transformation from ranges to numerico columns
# This feature extracts numbers from strings like "55-75" or "180+"
stats_extraction <- function(column) {
  nums <- str_extract_all(column, "\\d+")
  min_val <- sapply(nums, function(x) if(length(x) > 0) min(as.numeric(x)) else NA)
  max_val <- sapply(nums, function(x) if(length(x) > 0) max(as.numeric(x)) else NA)
  avg_val <- (min_val + max_val) / 2
  return(data.frame(min = min_val, max = max_val, avg = avg_val))
}

# We apply the function to the 4 main variables to create 12 new clean columns
stats_harvest <- stats_extraction(edible_plants$days_harvest)
stats_germ <- stats_extraction(edible_plants$days_germination)
stats_temp_germ <- stats_extraction(edible_plants$temperature_germination)
stats_temp_grow <- stats_extraction(edible_plants$temperature_growing)

edible_plants_clean <- edible_plants_clean %>%
  mutate(
    days_harvest_min = stats_harvest$min,
    days_harvest_max = stats_harvest$max,
    days_harvest_avg = stats_harvest$avg,
    days_germ_min = stats_germ$min,
    days_germ_max = stats_germ$max,
    days_germ_avg = stats_germ$avg,
    temp_germ_min = stats_temp_germ$min,
    temp_germ_max = stats_temp_germ$max,
    temp_germ_avg = stats_temp_germ$avg,
    temp_grow_min = stats_temp_grow$min,
    temp_grow_max = stats_temp_grow$max,
    temp_grow_avg = stats_temp_grow$avg
  )
```

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 4. Empyt values management
# We replace Nas with categorical variables so we do not loss information in the charts
edible_plants_clean <- edible_plants_clean %>%
  mutate(
    soil = replace_na(soil, "not specified"),
    nutrients = replace_na(nutrients, "not specified")
  )
```

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 5. Changes verification
# We compare the number of columns of the original model vs the new one
cat("Original columns:", ncol(edible_plants), "\n")
cat("Columns after cleaning:", ncol(edible_plants_clean), "\n")

# We show the first rows of the new added columns
edible_plants_clean %>% 
  select(common_name, starts_with("days_harvest")) %>% 
  head() %>%
  kable() %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
```

```{r, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# Type summary of all the columns of the dataset
glimpse(edible_plants)
glimpse(edible_plants_clean)
```

```{r, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# Numeric summary of all the columns of the database
skim(edible_plants)
skim(edible_plants_clean)
```

# Graph creation

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Propuesta de Dani
edible_plants_clean

pp<- edible_plants_clean %>%
  select(days_germ_avg, temp_germ_avg, temp_grow_avg, cultivation)

plot<- pp %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(days_germ_avg),
    !is.na(temp_germ_avg),
    !is.na(temp_grow_avg),
    !is.na(cultivation)
  )

graph1<-  plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = temp_grow_avg, y = days_germ_avg, ,color=cultivation))  + geom_point(size=1.2,alpha=0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Temperature of growing in relation with the days of germination by cultive",
    subtitle = "Edible plants dataset",
    x = "Temp of growing (cº)",
    y = "Days of germination",
    color = "Cultive"
  ) + theme_minimal() +  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE)

graph1
```


```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Propuesta de Dani
edible_plants_clean

pp<- edible_plants_clean %>%
  select(sunlight, nutrients, season, energy)

plot<- pp %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(sunlight),
    !is.na(nutrients),
    !is.na(season),
    !is.na(energy)
  )

graph2<-  plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = nutrients, y = energy, fill = season)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Nutrients in function of energy neccessary to grow",
    subtitle = "Edible plants dataset",
    x = "Nutrients",
    y = "Energy neccesary (Jls)",
    color = "Season"
  ) + theme_minimal()

graph2
```




```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Propuesta de Dani
edible_plants_clean

pp<- edible_plants_clean %>%
  select(preferred_ph_upper, temp_germ_avg, season, water,sunlight)

plot<- pp %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(preferred_ph_upper),
    !is.na(temp_germ_avg),
    !is.na(season),
    !is.na(water),
    !is.na(sunlight)
  )

graph3<-  plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(
    x = preferred_ph_upper,
    y = temp_germ_avg,
    color = sunlight
  )) +
  geom_point(size = 1.9, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    title = "Based on sunlight and amount water required, how the PH affects the temperature of germination",
    x = "Preferred ph (upper)",
    y = "Temperature of germination (Cº)",
    color = "Sunlight"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~ water) +
  theme_minimal()

graph3
```



```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Propuesta de Dani
edible_plants_clean

pp<- edible_plants_clean %>%
  select(cultivation, temp_germ_avg, temperature_class, water,sunlight)

plot<- pp %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(cultivation),
    !is.na(temperature_class)
  )

graph4 <- plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cultivation, fill = temperature_class)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of cultives in function of temperature class",
    subtitle = "Edible plants dataset",
    x = "Cultivation type",
    y = "Number",
    color = "Temperature"
  )

graph4

#PREGUNTAR AL PROFESOR COMO ARREGLARLO
```


```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Propuesta de Dani
edible_plants_clean

pp<- edible_plants_clean %>%
  select(days_harvest_avg, days_germ_avg, temperature_class, water,sunlight)

plot<- pp %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(days_harvest_avg),
    !is.na(days_germ_avg),
    !is.na(temperature_class)
  )

graph5 <-  plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = days_harvest_avg, y = days_germ_avg, ,color=temperature_class))  + geom_point(size=1.2,alpha=0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Days of harvest in function of germination days by temperature class",
    subtitle = "Edible plants dataset",
    x = "Days of harvest",
    y = "Days of germination",
    color = "Temperature"
  ) + theme_minimal() +  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE)

graph5

#PREGUNTAR AL PROFESOR COMO ARREGLARLO
```
